_README: Copy this file to ~/.claude/plan-tango/config.json and edit. CLI flags ALWAYS override these values. Validation runs on load — invalid values cause /plan-tango:tango to abort with a clear error.
_comment_model: Codex model name (e.g. gpt-5.5, gpt-5.3-codex-spark). null = let Codex choose its own default from ~/.codex/config.toml.
_comment_effort: Reasoning effort: none | minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh. Lower = faster + cheaper. Default high.
_comment_max_iter: Default iteration cap (1..12). Hard cap is 12 — config can not exceed it. Continue-prompt may raise it up to 12 mid-run.
_comment_thread_mode: continue (default) — first iteration opens a Codex thread, later iterations resume it (cheaper, faster, cleaner Codex panel; uses an explicit reset-prompt at iter >= 2 to limit anchor bias). fresh — each iteration is an independent thread (use --fresh-each to override per-run).
_comment_final_check: never | always. never (default): skip the Opus final-check after Codex convergence. always: run the Opus sanity-check on every converged-* status. Deprecated values auto and force are accepted but auto-migrated to never and always respectively with a one-line warning.
_comment_lenient: true = stop on no critical/major instead of strict ALLOW.
_comment_service_tier: null | fast | flex. fast = OpenAI priority processing tier (~1.5x speed, +$). Requires features.fast_mode=true in ~/.codex/config.toml (default in current Codex CLI).
_comment_codex_profile: Name of a [profiles.<name>] section in ~/.codex/config.toml. Loaded BEFORE per-call -c overrides; canonical config (effort, service_tier, model) still wins on conflict.
_comment_extra_codex_config: Array of literal '-c key=value' strings, applied AFTER profile but BEFORE canonical fields. Use for codex flags plan-tango does not surface natively. Managed keys (model_reasoning_effort, service_tier, model) here are silently overridden by the canonical fields above.
_comment_quiet: true = suppress per-iteration progress lines (snapshot/spawn/apply prints in Phase C). Phase E final report (stats + caught-and-fixed list) is ALWAYS printed regardless of this flag — quiet mutes only iteration-level chatter, not the final summary. Bash IN/OUT panels are rendered by Claude Code itself and unaffected by this flag.
_comment_severity_aware: true (default) = stop on polish-only verdicts (BLOCK with only minor/nit findings, zero critical/major) without running another corrective iter; sets status=converged-with-polish (or converged-lenient if --lenient also set), populates state.polish_advisory, lists findings as advisory in §6 of the Phase E report. false = revert to legacy behavior (always run corrective iter on any BLOCK).
_comment_verbose_report: false (default) = render only §1 header, §2 stats block, §4 caught-and-fixed list (and §6 polish-advisory when applicable). true = also render §3 per-iter convergence table and §5 narrative. CLI flag --verbose-report overrides per-run.
_comment_update_check: true (default) = at the end of every plan-tango run, check GitHub for a newer release tag and print a one-line update notice if one is available. Throttled to once per 7 days, fully silent on network failure or non-semver tags. No CLI flag — config-only opt-out. false = never check the remote.
